name: Pampushky
recipeIngredient: 5 cups Unbleached All-Purpose Flour (up to 6 may be needed)
1 cup Rose Preserve or Any Dry-Fruit Preserve
recipeInstructions:
1. Combine the sugar and water, then sprinkle with the yeast. Let stand until soft.
2. Heat the milk to lukewarm, and add the milk and 1/4 cup of flour to the yeast mixture. Beat well, cover, and allow to rise until light and bubbly (about 10 minutes).
3. In another bowl, cream the butter and sugar. Beat eggs and egg yolks together, blending well. Combine with the sugar-butter mixture, beating thoroughly until the eggs are pale white.
4. Add lemon zest, vanilla, and yeast mixture to the butter-egg mixture. Mix in 4 cups of flour. If the dough seems loose, add a little more, but it should be soft.
5. Knead by hand for about 10 minutes. Replace in a greased bowl, turning once to grease the top, and cover with a damp towel. Set in a warm place until doubled in bulk.
6. Punch down, knead a few more times, and allow to rise again until the dough is doubled.
7. Divide the dough into 4 parts. On a lightly floured surface, roll one part into a rectangle about 1/4-inch thick, turning once or twice during rolling for uniform thickness.
8. Dust with flour sparingly. Place rose preserve in evenly spaced intervals on the dough, or cut with a 2-1/2-inch cutter to form impressions and place filling in each.
9. Roll another portion out to the same thickness, gently covering the first, overlapping slightly.
10. Cut circles with the cutter. Place them on a lightly floured cookie sheet and allow to rise until double in size. Repeat until all dough is used, rolling out scraps last.
11. Heat oil or shortening to 375°F (190°C) in a deep fryer or wide skillet. Test temperature by frying bread; it should bubble and turn golden quickly without smoking.
12. Fry 5-6 pampushky at a time. Do not crowd as this lowers the temperature, causing doughnuts to absorb too much grease.
13. When one side is golden, flip with a spoon to fry the other side. Dough will puff up in frying.
14. Remove and drain on paper towels. Sprinkle lightly with powdered sugar when slightly cooled.
15. Serve with tea.
